topics: Continuous time and discrete time systems
Properties of systems: time invariance, linearity, causality, systems with and without memory, and stability
Convolution integral and convolution sum (graphical and any one matrix method)
Fourier transform: existence and properties of continuous time Fourier transform
Significance of Fourier transform and difference from Fourier series; energy and power spectral density
Characterization of LTI systems: differential equation representation of continuous time LTI systems
Transfer function representation of differential equation in Laplace domain
Modeling of LTI systems: electrical and translational mechanical system transfer function model
Impulse train sampling, sampling theorem, and aliasing effect; zero-order and first-order hold circuits
Z-transform: stability and causality conditions using ROC; characterization of difference equations using Z-transform
Pulse transfer function and impulse response of discrete-time systems
Delay operator and block diagram representation
Direct form, cascade, and parallel representations
Discrete Fourier series: Fourier representation of discrete time signals and properties
Discrete Time Fourier Transform: properties and frequency response of simple DT systems
